items.title: IBM just cut its outlook. Why its stock is bouncing higher anyway.
items.description: IBM’s official earnings report comes after a bruising profit warning last week.

items.title: ServiceNow’s stock rises as earnings show momentum in cybersecurity
items.description: Against a gloomy backdrop for software sentiment, ServiceNow just topped revenue expectations.

items.title: The Treasury market touches a worrying milestone not seen since 2007
items.description: There are a host of challenges at work as the ‘long’ 30-year Treasury yield logs its longest stretch above 5% in 19 years

items.title: Global oil prices settle at 6-week high after topping $95 a barrel, as hopes dim for de-escalation of Iran war
items.description: Oil prices settled at six-week highs on Wednesday after the U.S. struck Iran for the 11th night in a row and President Donald Trump warned that the U.S. will bomb Iranian bridges and power plants if T

items.title: We have $8 million in traditional IRAs. Should we tap them to buy a house — and take the tax hit?
items.description: “We would rather pay taxes upfront than pay mortgage interest.”

items.title: ‘Owning a pet has become a luxury’ — vets worry about higher prices and shrinking pet population, survey finds
items.description: A Morgan Stanley survey shows that U.S. vet practices are growing sales by raising prices as they contend with fewer visits —a formula that analysts say is unsustainable.

items.title: My second husband and I have kids from previous relationships. Should my house go to him or my children if I die first?
items.description: “We live in a house that I purchased entirely with money I had before we were married.”

items.title: More than 70% of recent home buyers were counting on mortgage rates to drop. Now they’re stuck.
items.description: Refinancing to a lower rate isn’t an option for many homeowners, who now face “financially unsustainable” mortgage payments.

items.title: The bull market faces higher likelihood of a Fed rate hike as Iran crisis intensifies
items.description: Treasury yields are approaching the highest levels seen since the Iran war started in February as oil price rises and odds of a Fed rate hike narrow.

items.title: The super rich use 401(k)s and IRAs to sidestep taxes on millions of dollars. This proposed law would cut them off.
items.description: More than 200 individuals had a total of more than $85 billion in tax-sheltered retirement accounts
